commands.review_summary:
Adds DeepSeek dashboard amount/cost parsing, platform usage fetches, UsageSnapshot plumbing, inline menu dashboard rows, and parser/fetcher tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect: source inspection shows the provider awaits the optional summary before returning balance, and the timeout task's nil result does not end the group. I did not run live network reproduction because this read-only review has no DeepSeek credentials.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor/maintainer follow-up for real behavior proof, credential-scope approval, and the non-blocking summary fix before merge.

target.title: DeepSeek: add web-session usage and cost summary
target.body:
## Summary

Add a DeepSeek web-session usage/cost summary alongside the existing DeepSeek balance display.

This uses the DeepSeek dashboard endpoints observed from the web console:

- `https://platform.deepseek.com/api/v0/usage/amount?month=<month>&year=<year>`
- `https://platform.deepseek.com/api/v0/usage/cost?month=<month>&year=<year>`

The existing balance endpoint remains unchanged on `https://api.deepseek.com/user/balance`.

## What changed

- Adds defensive DeepSeek amount/cost response models and aggregation parser.
- Fetches usage/cost summary only as an optional dashboard/session enhancement.
- Shows compact DeepSeek usage dashboard data:
  - Today tokens/cost
  - This month tokens/cost
  - Request count
  - Top model
  - Cache-hit input, cache-miss input, and output token breakdown
- Keeps the existing DeepSeek balance display unchanged.
- Silently hides the usage/cost section on 401/403/network error/schema drift/parse failure.

## Safety

- Does not persist raw usage rows.
- Does not log or render Authorization headers, cookies, bearer tokens, `x-ds-trace-id`, account IDs, emails, or raw credential material.
- Test fixtures use synthetic redacted data only.

## Testing

- `swift build` — passed
- `swift test --filter "DeepSeekUsageCostParserTests"` — passed
- `swift test --filter "DeepSeekUsageFetcherTests"` — passed
- `swift test --filter "MiniMax"` — passed
- `swift test` — passed, 2956 tests
- `./Scripts/lint.sh lint` — passed, 0 violations
target.author: yuxin-qiao
